decisions.decision: Move multi-variable clique building from server.py into the inference module compiler so conditional-context-derived cliques are part of the compiled artifact, not synthetic server helpers
alternatives_rejected: Leave synthetic clique building in server.py — duplicates inference-concern logic and prevents the compiler from computing true treewidth
Build a fully general tree decomposition algorithm — out of scope for skeleton; current model only has conditional-context-implied interactions, not arbitrary factor graphs

decisions.decision: Compute actual junction tree width from multi-variable cliques in the compiler and expose it in the compile artifact, replacing the hardcoded width=0
alternatives_rejected: Keep width=0 for all markets — misleads observability and prevents eligibility gating
Compute width lazily on query — compile-time width is needed for eligibility determination before any query is issued

decisions.decision: Add a max_treewidth bound to EngineConfig and use it to determine exact_eligible in the compiler. Markets exceeding the bound are marked ineligible with a descriptive reason.
alternatives_rejected: Hardcode the treewidth limit — makes testing and future configuration changes harder
Skip eligibility checking entirely — the existing exact_eligible field and eligibility_reason already exist but are always true; this is the right time to make them meaningful

decisions.decision: Remove duplicated server.py helpers (build_market_cliques, clique_state_count, parse_context_key_variable_ids, estimate_market_engine_memory_bytes) after migrating their logic into the inference module
alternatives_rejected: Keep both server and inference versions — duplication leads to divergence and defeats the extraction goal of T548

decisions.decision: Pass conditional_marginals and market outcome metadata into the compiler so it can build context-aware cliques without reaching into server globals
alternatives_rejected: Have the compiler read MARKETS and CONDITIONAL_MARGINALS globals directly — violates the compile boundary; compiler should be a pure function of its inputs
Add a market-state provider protocol — over-abstraction for this skeleton phase

steps.task: Extend CurrentModelCompiler.compile_artifact to accept an optional market_outcomes_by_variable parameter (mapping variable_id -> outcome_count) and build multi-variable cliques from conditional_marginals context keys instead of only singleton cliques. Compute junction_tree_width as max(clique_size - 1) across all cliques.

Add max_treewidth field to EngineConfig (default: a reasonable bound like 8) and wire it into the compiler so that artifacts with junction_tree_width > max_treewidth have exact_eligible=False with eligibility_reason='treewidth_exceeds_bound'.

Add context-key parsing and clique-state-count computation to the inference module (either in current_model.py or a new helpers file) so the compiler can derive multi-variable cliques without depending on server.py helpers.

Update compile_market_for_inference in server.py to pass the new market_outcomes_by_variable data to the compiler and remove the now-redundant build_market_cliques, clique_state_count, parse_context_key_variable_ids, and estimate_market_engine_memory_bytes helpers from server.py.
Update __init__.py exports and any downstream imports (server.py, tests) to reflect the new inference module surface.

Add unit tests for: multi-variable clique building from conditional contexts, treewidth computation, bounded-treewidth eligibility gating (both eligible and ineligible cases), and context-key parsing.
Run full test suite (python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_bayes_market*.py') and verify all 363+ tests pass with no regressions.

children.title: Multi-variable clique building and treewidth computation
children.description: Extend CurrentModelCompiler to build multi-variable cliques from conditional_marginals context keys, compute junction_tree_width as max(clique_size - 1), and add bounded-treewidth eligibility checking via max_treewidth in EngineConfig. Covers steps 1-3.

children.title: Migrate server synthetic helpers to inference module
children.description: Update compile_market_for_inference in server.py to pass market outcome metadata to the compiler. Remove build_market_cliques, clique_state_count, parse_context_key_variable_ids, and estimate_market_engine_memory_bytes from server.py. Update exports and imports. Covers steps 4-5.

children.title: Bounded-treewidth test coverage
children.description: Add unit tests for multi-variable clique building, treewidth computation, eligibility gating, and context-key parsing. Verify full test suite passes with no regressions. Covers steps 6-7.
